slides.heading: The check every RAG demo skips
slides.body: Building a RAG system is the demo. The verification layer is what actually ships it.

slides.heading: The answer is only a first draft
slides.body: Question, retrieve, generate: that is the easy part. Four checks decide whether to trust the answer, each running at a different time.

slides.heading: The cheap gate
slides.body: Is the answer backed by what we retrieved? If not, refuse rather than guess. Cheap enough to run on every single answer.

slides.heading: The second opinion
slides.body: A slower, fuller read of grounding, coherence and policy, spent only where the cheap gate hesitated.

slides.heading: The offline judge
slides.body: Reread a sample of yesterday's answers to catch the slow drift the per-answer checks aren't looking for.

slides.heading: The simulation
slides.body: Run each new release against hundreds of stand-in customers before a single real one meets it.

slides.heading: The verifier answers an easier question
slides.body: None of them has to solve the model's whole problem. Checking an answer is a narrower job than writing one, and a cheaper one.
